facebook:
The NZ Age Group Swimming Championships are just days away (April 8-12), and for those lining up on the blocks, this week is about one thing: arriving ready.

The athletes who perform best on race day are not the ones who trained the hardest in the final week. They are the ones who trusted their preparation, dialled in their race-day routine, and showed up in gear that felt like a second skin.

If you are still undecided on your race suit, now is the time to sort it. Your swimwear should be the last thing on your mind when the starter goes. Velocx Italian performance swimwear is engineered to move with your body, reduce drag, and give you that extra edge when every tenth of a second counts.

For those heading to The Mount Swim later in April or building toward triathlon season, this is also prime time to get your open water kit dialled. The water is still warm, the swells are manageable, and there is no better training window.

Tip for this week: focus on your turns. Clean, tight turns in a short-course pool can take seconds off your time without changing your aerobic fitness at all. Film yourself from the side and watch your entry angle.

Train smart. Race hard. Wear Italian.
